message:
🌱 opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution. (#1875)

* 🌱 Add opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution. mission

* 🌱 Add opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution. mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add merged OCI module installer PR #2668 to opentofu-308

Add the main OCI module installer PR opentofu/opentofu#2668 (merged)
to the sourceUrls for the OCI registries mission.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opentofu-308-support-oci-registries-for-provider-and-module-distribution",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",

+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.",
+    "description": "Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.. Requested by 172+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opentofu setup",
+        "description": "Verify your opentofu version and configuration:\n```bash\ntofu version\n```\nThis feature requires a working opentofu installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opentofu configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant opentofu configuration:\nRight now OpenTF relies on a specific registry implementation to host providers and modules."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.",
+        "description": "There are a few registries out there:\n\n- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry\n- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen\n- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg\n\nAs well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.\n\nOCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.\n\nI would be interested in finding a way to ensure that we're not switching to OCI, but instead adding it as an\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "There are a few registries out there:\n\n- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry\n- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen\n- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg\n\nAs well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.\n\nOCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "opentofu-308-support-oci-registries-for-provider-and-module-distribution",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "opentofu: Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.",
+    "description": "Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.. Requested by 172+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current opentofu deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your opentofu version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nhelm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working opentofu installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review opentofu configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nkubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu\n```\nRight now OpenTF relies on a specific registry implementation to host providers and modules."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.",
+        "description": "There are a few registries out there:\n\n- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry\n- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen\n- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg\n\nAs well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.\n\nOCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.\n\nI would be interested in finding a way to ensure that we're not switching to OCI, but instead adding it as an\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu\nkubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support OCI registries for provider and module distribution.\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "There are a few registries out there:\n\n- https://github.com/boring-registry/boring-registry\n- https://github.com/outsideris/citizen\n- https://github.com/matthewjohn/terrareg\n\nAs well as Spacelift and other TACOS (env0, scalr etc) also providing private module registries.\n\nOCI does seem like a good fit, but there is an existing ecosystem for modules that we can't forget about here.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
